Tests if the `typeof` operator correctly identifies boolean values.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion script that verifies the data type of the boolean values `true` and `false`. It uses the `assert` function to check if both `true` and `false` are of the type `boolean`. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on type validation for boolean literals. It is likely used in a testing context to ensure that the JavaScript environment correctly identifies boolean values.
